notes: Large Opening Day slate (~15 games). 10 of 15 games captured from box score data. Teams represented in results: BOS, DET, LAA, PHI, MIN, BAL, HOU, MIL, CLE, LAD, PIT, STL, CIN, SEA, TEX. Jacob Misiorowski (MIL) 11K franchise record. Chase DeLauter (CLE) 2 HR in MLB debut.
Friday slate. Cam Schlittler (NYY) MLB debut 5.1 IP 1H 0ER 8K. Sandy Alcantara (MIA) 6th opening day start. Mookie Betts 3-run HR. Shea Langeliers 2 HR off different pitchers.

Saturday slate with 4 extra-inning games. Shea Langeliers grand slam 420ft (3rd HR in 2 games). Luis Robert Jr. walk-off 3-run HR in 11th. JJ Wetherholt walk-off 2-run single in 10th. Royce Lewis 2-run HR + SB.

Sunday slate. Yandy Diaz 5-for-6 career-high. Colson Montgomery grand slam in CWS loss. Christian Yelich pinch-hit 3-run HR (first career PH HR). Owen Caissie pinch-hit walk-off 2-run HR. Alex Bregman 2 solo HRs as a Cub.
Monday slate. Miguel Vargas 6 RBI (grand slam + sac fly + RBI single). Corbin Carroll 3-run HR + triple. Jose Altuve 4-for-? w/2 HR (15th career multi-HR game). COL 14 TOR 5 blowout.

Tuesday slate. Shohei Ohtani two-way: 6 IP 1H 0ER 6K pitching + 1-for-3 2BB batting. Oneil Cruz 2 HR (444ft + 403ft). Willy Adames 4-for-5 near cycle. Logan Webb got run support (SF 9 SD 3). First day with widespread card_boost data.

Thursday travel day — only 3 games played. CWS vs TOR postponed (rain). Cole Ragans (KCR) 6 IP 8K but took L. Matt Olson (ATL) RS 5.6 highest individual RS (HR + 2 doubles in 17-2 blowout). Taj Bradley (MIN) 7 IP 1ER 9K earned W. Jordan Lawlar first career HR (126 PA). Twins broke game open with 3 HR in 9th (Wallner, Clemens, Bell). Juan Soto (NYM) most popular at 5.6k drafts but RS only 0.4. Willy Adames (SF) RS -0.8 with +3.0x boost drafted 2.6k times — notable bust. Winner (military) used Matt Olson in slot 1 — key differentiator from ranks 2-8 who put Ragans in slot 1. Heavy ATL stack dominated top lineups.

Friday slate with 15 games. Bryan Woo (SEA) RS 7.3 highest individual RS with complete game shutout. Sonny Gray (BOS) most drafted 3x at 1.9k. Michael Lorenzen (CIN) RS -6.4 notable bust (-32.0 total_value with +3.0x boost). Aaron Judge 2 HR 4 RBI. Ghost players dominated HV leaderboard — 8 of top 14 had fewer than 10 drafts.

Saturday slate with 15 games (14 complete, 1 doubleheader). Top 8 winning lineups captured. Cubs-Guardians postponed. Jesús Luzardo dominated top lineups (most popular, highest value combination). Three shutouts: HOU 11-0 over OAK, CIN 2-0 over TEX, NYM 9-0 over SF.

Monday slate (13 games). LAD dominated TOR 14-2 — Dodgers stack swept the leaderboard: Ohtani (RS 2.0, 4.9k drafts), Freeman (RS 4.0, 53 drafts), Hernandez (RS 5.2, 3 drafts), Pages (RS 2.2, 1.7k drafts), Rushing (RS 4.7, 1 draft), Wrobleski (RS 3.9). Rank 1 (killacam31, 71.76) used Ohtani/Freeman/Tucker/Hernandez/Rushing — pure LAD+HOU stack. WSH beat STL 9-6: ghost player sweep from James Wood (RS 5.5, 3 drafts), Brady House (RS 4.9, 2 drafts), Luis Garcia Jr. (RS 0.1, minimal drafts) — rank 2 (jcurns, 71.19) ran full WSH stack + Littell. José Soriano (LAA) RS 8.2 — best pitcher of the day, 142 drafts, LAA beat ATL 6-2. Boost traps: Brayan Bello (BOS) RS 0.3 at +3.0x (2.1k drafts) — MIL beat BOS 8-6; Vlad Guerrero Jr (TOR) RS -0.3 at +3.0x — LAD crushed TOR; Ronald Acuña Jr (ATL) RS -0.4 at +3.0x — LAA won 6-2. Most popular: Ohtani 4.9k drafts appeared in every top-12 lineup but slotted lower as Hernandez was the true HV stud. Ghost edge: Hernandez (3 drafts, RS 5.2), Wood (3 drafts, RS 5.5), Rushing (1 draft, RS 4.7), Contreras (1 draft, RS 5.0) all HV at near-zero ownership.
